title: Incremental client cutover plan
objective: Define a phased client migration from Firebase-backed integrations to platform APIs with explicit rollback switches per client surface.

principles: Prefer server-side feature flags and API gateway routing over client redeploy-only toggles.
Keep Firebase read paths available in shadow mode during early phases for parity checks.
Use per-surface rollback switches with independent activation to avoid global blast radius.
globalSafeguards: Release freeze window and on-call coverage for each cutover phase.
SLO-based guardrails: error rate, latency, and data parity thresholds must remain within agreed bounds.
Automated rollback trigger if critical user journey failures exceed threshold for >10 minutes.

cutoverSwitches.purpose: Route login/claims resolution to platform auth endpoints.
Route customer/contractor/asset operations to platform APIs.
Route activity summary/detail calls to platform analytics endpoints.
Move request list/detail/status transitions to platform APIs.
Move request history/note reads+writes to platform APIs.
Enable websocket/SSE replacement for Firestore realtime behavior.
Route request and timeline operations to platform API.
Use signed upload/download URLs in place of Firebase storage.
Move OTP/token registration and validation to platform endpoints.
Route request and workflow operations to platform API.
Move to signed media upload/download flow.
Move OTP/push-token and identity verification operations.

phasedSequence.name: Readiness and shadowing
actions: Implement API clients/adapters in each client without user-visible behavior change.
Enable shadow reads/writes where feasible to compare Firebase vs platform outputs.
Baseline telemetry dashboards per surface before first traffic migration.
exitCriteria: Parity checks pass for core payload fields and status transitions.
No P0/P1 defects open in migrated pathways.

phasedSequence.name: Internal and low-risk cutover
actions: Cut over web-admin auth and low-risk read operations first.
Cut over portal read paths for request lists/history with fallback retained.
Keep mobile write flows on Firebase until API error budget is proven.
phasedSequence.trafficStrategy: 10% canary -> 50% -> 100% per surface after 24h healthy soak.

phasedSequence.name: Transactional write cutover
actions: Enable request status transitions and note writes through platform APIs.
Enable mobile request write flows, then media and identity subflows.
Activate realtime replacement channel for portal if required by UX parity.
phasedSequence.trafficStrategy: Progressive cohort rollout with per-surface independent rollback.

phasedSequence.name: Firebase retirement
actions: Disable Firebase-dependent code paths behind flags once stability gates pass.
Archive migration parity reports and incident logs.
Remove dead code and legacy SDK dependencies in final cleanup release.
exitCriteria: All surface switches stable at API mode for two full release cycles.
Rollback switches unused for 14 consecutive days.

triggerConditions: Critical path failures in request submission/approval/history retrieval.
Sustained API 5xx above threshold or p95 latency breach.
Data integrity mismatch detected in parity monitor.
steps: Disable impacted surface switch(es) only; do not globally disable all clients unless cross-surface incident.
Confirm traffic restoration to Firebase path and validate user journey smoke tests.
Create incident record with timeline, failure mode, and remediation owner.
Re-enter canary stage after fix and targeted validation.

requiredChecks: Client smoke tests for login/request/note/image/invoice critical paths.
Contract tests for migrated endpoints and payload compatibility.
Observability checks for latency, error rate, and notification delivery.
